data.title: Delphi Digital predicts Perp DEXs will surpass Traditional Finance by 2026, despite lower trading volumes currently.
data.description: Decentralized exchanges, known as DEXs, are currently managing only a small portion of the trading volume when compared to traditional finance (TradFi) investments. However, the technology and systems supporting DEXs are advancing rapidly to match the pace of the sluggish and disjointed centralized trading platforms.

data.title: ADAS Simulation Market to reach $9.66B by 2032 with 14.3% CAGR, up from $3.79B in 2025 | MarketsandMarkets™.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, as of January 14, 2026, it has been reported that the ADAS simulation market is anticipated to witness significant growth. Research from MarketsandMarkets™ reveals that the market is set to increase from USD 3.79 billion in 2025 to USD 9.66 billion by 2032, indicating a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 14.3%. The study includes extensive market data with 174 tables and 90 figures incorporated across 298 pages.

data.title: WSPN introduces stablecoin-based trade finance for JOC Australia Pty Ltd with asset-backed digital bills of lading.
data.description: On January 14, 2026, WSPN, a prominent provider of regulated stablecoin infrastructure based in Tortola, British Virgin Islands, revealed a significant development. They launched stablecoin-denominated trade finance backed by assets to assist JOC Australia Pty Ltd, an Australian commodity exporter. This deployment marks a milestone for WSPN and signifies their commitment to providing innovative financial solutions.

data.title: By 2026, Perp DEXs projected to surpass traditional finance, despite handling only a fraction of its trading volume.
data.description: Decentralized exchanges (DEXs) currently account for only a small portion of trading compared to traditional finance (TradFi) investments. However, their technology is rapidly improving to compete with the inefficient and fragmented centralized trading platforms.

data.title: DataCamp to offer free AI training to 1M teachers & students globally by 2026, in collaboration with the Mark Cuban Foundation.
data.description: A major announcement was made in New York as DataCamp committed to providing free artificial intelligence training to one million teachers and students across the globe by 2026. This initiative includes an enhanced collaboration with the Mark Cuban Foundation.

data.title: Greenland chooses Denmark over the US ahead of high-stakes White House meeting. Greenland PM announces decision, amid Trump's push for takeover.
data.description: Foreign ministers from Greenland and Denmark are scheduled to meet with U.S. Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at the White House on Wednesday. This comes as President Donald Trump continues his efforts to acquire the self-governed Danish territory. The meeting is crucial, involving Greenland's Foreign Minister Vivian Motzfeldt and Danish counterpart Lars Lokke Rasmussen. Greenland's Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen recently stated that the island prefers Denmark over the U.S. during a press conference with Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen. Nielsen emphasized, "We are in a geopolitical crisis, and if we must decide between the United States and Denmark now, we choose Denmark." Trump's strong interest in Greenland has resurfaced, prompting discussions on its potential acquisition.

data.title: Ilbok Lee retires from Magnachip Board of Directors, effective Jan 14, 2026. Joined in 2011, served in various roles.
data.description: In Seoul, South Korea, Magnachip Semiconductor Corporation (NYSE: MX) has disclosed that Ilbok Lee plans to retire from the Board of Directors, with the retirement set to take place at the next scheduled Board meeting on January 14, 2026. Dr. Lee, who has been on the Board since August 2011, has held various roles, including Chair of the Nominating and Corporate committees.

data.title: Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) vows to cover its own data center power costs to prevent local electricity bills from surging due to its operations. Commitment includes collaborating with utilities to reflect its share of grid strain, creating jobs in local communities.
data.description: Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ: MSFT) recently announced its commitment to a "community-first" approach in its AI infrastructure expansion, aiming to prevent local electricity bills from increasing due to its operations. The company revealed its plans to prioritize being a responsible neighbor in areas where it constructs, owns, and operates data centers. Microsoft will work with local utilities to ensure its payments accurately reflect its impact on the local grid, ultimately preventing elevated energy costs for local residents. Additionally, the tech giant pledged to generate employment opportunities in communities hosting its data centers and reduce water consumption in these facilities. This initiative underscores Microsoft's dedication to sustainable growth and positive community relations. For further details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kennametal (NYSE: KMT) hosting Q2 2026 earnings call on Feb 4, 2026. Press release and presentation available on website.
data.description: Kennametal Inc. (NYSE: KMT) is set to conduct its Q2 FY2026 earnings call on February 4, 2026, in Pittsburgh. This event will provide insights into the company's financial performance in the second quarter. The press release and accompanying presentation will be accessible on Kennametal's website prior to the market opening on the same day. Investors and stakeholders can gain valuable information regarding the company's earnings, strategies, and future outlook during this call.

data.title: emagine acquires Waada The Movement, enhancing advisory services. This Denmark-Netherlands deal targets business transformation.
data.description: On January 14, 2026, emagine, a company based in Copenhagen, Denmark, revealed their plan to acquire Waada The Movement, a consultancy located in Delft, Netherlands. Waada The Movement specializes in business transformation, data, and software development. The acquisition agreement has been finalized between the two companies.

data.title: 300 students from West Virginia will participate in a School Choice Showcase at Charleston Coliseum & Convention Center on Jan. 28, 2026, to share their love for their schools.
data.description: On January 28, 2026, 300 students will convene at the Charleston Coliseum & Convention Center in Charleston, West Virginia. Their purpose is to express appreciation for school choice and affection for their respective schools. The gathering aims to showcase the students' support and gratitude towards the educational options they have.
